install_tell: export STORAGE_LOCATION=$HOME/anythingllm && mkdir -p $STORAGE_LOCATION && touch "$STORAGE_LOCATION/.env" && docker run -d --rm -p 3001:3001 --cap-add SYS_ADMIN -v ${STORAGE_LOCATION}:/app/server/storage -v ${STORAGE_LOCATION}/.env:/app/server/.env -e STORAGE_DIR="/app/server/storage" mintplexlabs/anythingllm
misconfig_patterns: Documented quickstart exposes port 3001 with AUTH_TOKEN commented out -> fully unauth UI+API on the public internet
Single-user AUTH_TOKEN is the ONLY gate for non-multi-user installs and is opt-in; operators frequently skip it for 'internal' boxes that are actually internet-reachable
DISABLE_LOGIN / Simple SSO Passthrough intended for a fronting portal can leave the app open if the portal is bypassed (direct-to-3001)
AUTH_TOKEN containing a '#' is silently truncated by sanitizeValue() (issue #5036), weakening/breaking the intended password
Multi-user mode is irreversible and historically shipped privilege-escalation/RCE bugs for the default user role (CVE-2024-0765, CVE-2024-13060)

passive: http.title:"AnythingLLM | Your personal LLM trained on anything"
http.html:"AnythingLLM | Your personal LLM trained on anything"

active_probe.false_positive_check: The og:title/description "AnythingLLM | Your personal LLM trained on anything" is the verbatim vendor string and does not collide with generic LLM hosts; bare "AnythingLLM" can appear in blog posts, doc mirrors, and proxied embed widgets. To avoid the ~50% false positive: confirm with the unauth JSON contract — GET /api/ping must return exactly {"online":true} AND GET /api/system/custom-app-name must return a JSON object with the `customAppName` key. A doc/blog mirror or reverse-proxied marketing page will 404 these API routes. Distinguish a default-OPEN instance from an AUTH_TOKEN-protected one by GET /api/system/onboarding: a 200 with onboardingComplete plus a reachable workspace list = no AUTH_TOKEN gate; a 403/redirect on authenticated routes while /api/ping still answers = AUTH_TOKEN is set (surface fingerprinted, access not open).

shodan_dorks.strict: http.title:"AnythingLLM | Your personal LLM trained on anything"

deployment_tells: Server-side default port 3001 (SERVER_PORT=3001 is the only active line in docker/.env.example); collapsed React SPA served from / with title 'AnythingLLM | Your personal LLM trained on anything'
GET /api/ping -> {"online":true} is a near-zero-cost liveness/identity tell
Bundled docker-compose stands up app + Qdrant vector DB + optional nginx; SYS_ADMIN cap requested for in-app document parsing/OCR
Desktop (Electron) build binds localhost only; the Docker/cloud image is the internet-exposed variant
favicon at /favicon.png (png, not ico) referenced by both rel=icon and apple-touch-icon
pivot_paths: Read configured LLM provider + API keys via the unauth settings UI/API, then bill against the operator's upstream key (LLMjacking) — read-only confirmation via the provider/model dropdown state
Enumerate workspaces and list ingested documents (/api/system/local-files, workspace endpoints) to scope RAG corpus sensitivity without downloading content
Read /api/system/system-vectors for vector counts to gauge corpus size
Read agent/skill and system-prompt definitions to map the operator's internal business logic
Pull /api/system/custom-app-name + /api/system/logo + /api/system/is-default-logo for operator branding/attribution (custom logo = named tenant)
Read chat/conversation history per workspace for leaked secrets and PII
GET /api/setup-complete and /api/system/onboarding to confirm setup state and whether multi-user/auth has been enabled
vulnerabilities: CVE-2025-63390 / GHSA-m6qc-6c6w-6687 — authentication bypass on /api/workspaces (Moderate; affects ~v1.8.5)
CVE-2024-0765 / GHSA-qp7v-9hpx-hcp3 — default (non-admin) user RCE on multi-user instances (Critical; <=1.3.1 era)
CVE-2024-3279 / GHSA-rrpm-pxv9-75vx — improper access control / IDOR (Critical)
CVE-2024-5211 / GHSA-v9ph-8hf4-hgrx — path traversal bypassing manager role restrictions (Critical)
CVE-2024-10513 / GHSA-45p5-8q33-98hw — path traversal in document uploads (High)
CVE-2024-13059 / GHSA-866h-pm2w-j493 — path traversal -> arbitrary file write -> RCE in file upload; fixed in 1.3.1 (Critical)
CVE-2024-13060 / GHSA-mjvp-pg3h-wh59 — privilege escalation via default permissions, Docker (Moderate; fixed 1.3.1)
CVE-2024-3570 / GHSA-wx55-ppw9-52p9 — stored XSS in chat (Low)
auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: docker/.env.example (master) ships AUTH_TOKEN commented out: `# AUTH_TOKEN="hunter2" # This is the password to your application if remote hosting.` — alongside commented `# JWT_SECRET` ("Only needed if AUTH_TOKEN is set"), `# SIG_KEY`, `# SIG_SALT`. The only active line is `SERVER_PORT=3001`. The canonical quickstart in docker/HOW_TO_USE_DOCKER.md (`docker run -d --rm -p 3001:3001 --cap-add SYS_ADMIN -v ... mintplexlabs/anythingllm`) sets NO AUTH_TOKEN and NO password. Server behavior (server/endpoints/system.js): single-user login is enforced only when AUTH_TOKEN is present in env ("disabled if in development or AUTH_TOKEN is unset"). Multi-user mode is separate, explicitly opt-in, and irreversible. Therefore a user running the documented quickstart and exposing port 3001 gets a fully unauthenticated UI AND API. Auth exists but is OFF unless the AUTH_TOKEN env var (or multi-user mode) is set => configurable-default-off.
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM provider API keys (OpenAI/Anthropic/Azure/etc.) — stored in settings and readable/usable via the unauth UI/API when AUTH_TOKEN is unset
Ingested RAG documents and their text chunks (PII/PHI/proprietary corpora) via workspace + document endpoints
Workspace + agent/skill definitions revealing internal business logic and prompts
System settings including embedder/vector-DB/LLM provider configuration (custom-app-name, logo, env-derived config)
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
